the task is to compute the sum of absolute differences for each element in a sorted array, with a twist for every third element. the first step is to calculate the sum of absolute differences for all elements except the current one. the second step is to handle the special case where the index is a multiple of 3, in which case the sum of squared differences is computed instead. the third step is to ensure that the calculation is done efficiently for large arrays. the fourth step is to verify that the results meet the required constraints and formatting.

the first part of the solution involves calculating the sum of absolute differences for each element. this can be done by iterating through the array and for each element, calculating the absolute difference with all other elements. the second part of the solution involves handling the special case where the index is a multiple of 3. in this case, the sum of squared differences is calculated instead of the absolute differences. the third part of the solution involves ensuring that the calculation is done efficiently for large arrays, as the array can be up to 10^5 elements long. the fourth part of the solution involves verifying that the results meet the required constraints and formatting, including the repetition of the phrase "the pen is mightier than sword" exactly twice.
